ocr.text: In the analysis of various bacteria across different aquifers, E. coli reported values of 0.5 (0–120) in Karst-Fissured Aquifers, 0
(0–3000) in Porous Aquifers, 0 (0–1700) for Total, 1 (0–1600) in GA, 1 (0–900) for SA, and 0 (0–3000) for MU, with a p-Value
of <0.001. Salmonella spp. b showed values of 0 (0–0) in Karst-Fissured Aquifers, 0 (0–120) in Porous Aquifers, 0 (0–20) for
Total, 0 (0–8) in GA, 0 (0–7) for SA, and 0 (0–120) in MU, resulting in a p-Value of 0.638. Total coliforms a were measured at
30 (0–900) in Karst-Fissured Aquifers, 2 (0–6000) in Porous Aquifers, 0 (0–2000) for Total, 24 (0–6000) in GA, 35 (0–2000)
for SA, and 10 (0–6000) in MU, with a significant p-Value of <0.001. Enterococci a had values of 2.5 (0–50) in Karst-Fissured
Aquifers, 0 (0–900) in Porous Aquifers, 0 (0–500) for Total, 3 (0–300) in GA, 1 (0–500) for SA, and 0 (0–900) in MU, with a p-
Value of <0.001. In the second table, under the stimulus of cAMP 0.1 μM, LvsA- recorded < 0.1 (7) and DH1 was nd. For 1
μM, LvsA- also recorded < 0.1 (7) and DH1 was 100 ± 59 (3). At 10 μM, LvsA- measured 1 ± 5 (9) while DH1 showed 203 ±
